prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial is shown in black and white and appears to be an older television advertisement. A “tvdays.com” watermark is visible in the lower-right corner of the frames.

At the beginning, the image shows a framed portrait or displayed photograph of a young girl standing in a dress. She is posed with one hand raised. At the right edge of the frame, a large hand or finger points toward the image, suggesting someone is indicating or selecting the photograph.

The scene then changes to an adult man seated at a desk in a home or office-like room. Behind him are shelves with books and a curtained or patterned wall area. On the desk in front of him is a piece of electronic equipment, likely a tape recorder. The man smiles toward the camera while holding up a small photograph or card.

Next, the commercial cuts to a close-up of the equipment: a reel-to-reel tape recorder. Two reels are visible on the top surface, along with knobs, spindles, wires, and a control panel area. Papers and other desk items are partly visible around it.

The final frame shows a large RCA logo centered on a plain background. The logo is enclosed in a circle, with the letters “RCA” prominently displayed.
